climate.description: Temperate spring conditions in western Switzerland, ideal for road cycling but watch for showers.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: Hilly road sportive emulating the Tour de Romandie's punchy Jura and Alpine foothills, featuring rolling terrain, steep kicks, and lake views culminating near Lausanne.
course_description.signature_challenge: The 'Romandie Rollercoaster': 8500ft over 100 miles of Swiss climbs, blending endurance with explosive efforts akin to pro Queen stages.
suffering_zones: Jura Foothills: Early undulating climbs building fatigue over 2000-3000ft gain with false flats.
Alpine Approaches: Mid-route ascents toward Thyon-like summits, delivering 4000ft of relentless gradient.
Lausanne Finale: Hilly time trial-inspired finish with uphill sting to Olympic stadium overlooking Lake Geneva.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant hotels in Lausanne and along Lake Geneva; book early for spring demand.
logistics.transport: Single-day event in late April, centered in Lausanne with easy urban access and pro-level organization.

history.origin_story: Amateur sportive inspired by the prestigious Tour de Romandie stage race, bringing pro parcours to gran fondo enthusiasts in Romandie.

final_verdict.one_liner: Pro-caliber road suffering in postcard Switzerland—pure gran fondo gold.
